source.title: Roam-code
source.summary: Phase A first-investigation pressure: assess whether Roam-code's local semantic graph and architecture intelligence for AI coding agents should later inform one bounded local-first Roam-code spike for Directive Workspace without opening immediate adoption, runtime rollout, or broad tool comparison.
notes: Upstream Roam-code describes itself as an architectural intelligence layer for AI coding agents with a local semantic graph over symbols, dependencies, call graphs, architecture layers, git history, and runtime traces. Phase A / Phase 1 retains only source capture and preservation.
source.missionAlignmentHint: Phase A first-investigation pressure: preserve one concrete local-first architecture-intelligence source for agents before any spike planning starts.

mission.currentObjective: Build and operate a revenue-generating personal AI workspace that continuously strengthens its own capabilities through mission-driven ingestion, proof-backed adoption, and persistent orchestration.
usefulnessSignals: Useful means anything that materially improves:
capabilityLanes: Directive engine materialization - one reusable executable core that hosts can embed cleanly
Runtime operationalization (Runtime) - converting proven patterns into callable, bounded, measurable runtime behavior
Orchestration reliability (OpenClaw) - persistent, looping, memory-backed coordination across agents and sessions
Discovery as operational front door - mission-aware intake, routing, capability-gap detection, and review-cadence enforcement
Behavior-preserving transformation - same capability, better implementation (speed, cost, reliability, maintainability)
Evaluation quality - dimensional measurement (evaluators), not just binary gates
Unified runtime host (Mission Control) - coherent command surface, less fragmented tooling

openGaps.description: Canonical Directive engine surface is only partially materialized as one reusable executable core with clear lane boundaries and host-adapter seams
openGaps.currentState: A first engine slice exists, but real source intake still falls back to host helpers, Markdown-first assets, and manual post-decision lane handoff
openGaps.desiredState: Engine owns substantially more intake, routing, adaptation/improvement, proof, decision, and handoff state while Discovery, Runtime, and Architecture operate as clear Engine lanes

rationale: Matched open gap gap-directive-engine-materialization (rank 1) as the closest current mission pressure.
Recommended discovery because the source is promising but the active phased plan only authorizes capture and preservation in Phase A / Phase 1.
Meta-usefulness signal is present because the source may improve how agents understand repo structure before they open downstream work.
Fast-path is recommended because the current thread should stop at source capture, routing, and preservation.

extractedValue: Roam-code offers local semantic indexing and architecture-level graph queries for AI coding agents.
The source may later support one bounded local-first Architecture spike around repo understanding for agents.
excludedBaggage: repo-wide rollout, CI integration, and broad tool comparison

adaptationPlan.directiveOwnedForm: Mission-aware Discovery source case with explicit phase gating and later bounded-spike eligibility.
adaptedValue: Normalize the source into a Discovery-owned preserved case.
Keep later Architecture work gated behind Phase A / Phase 2.

improvementGoals: improve future repo-understanding question framing for agents
improvementPlan.intendedDelta: Make the Roam-code question explicit without opening the spike early.

proofPlan.objective: Confirm the source is captured, preserved, and phase-gated correctly for Phase A / Phase 1.
proofPlan.rollbackPrompt: Keep the candidate in Discovery monitor and do not open downstream work.

decision.summary: Preliminary engine decision: hold_in_discovery for Discovery so Phase A / Phase 1 can stop at source capture and preservation.

Keep the candidate in Discovery until Phase A / Phase 2 is explicitly opened.
